title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs server configuration, http/1.1, http/2, tls, and graceful shutdown from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Server Timeout Header Limits
steps.instruction:
Order the Server Timeout Header Limits Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Server Timeout Header Limits.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Server Timeout Header Limits assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Server Timeout Header Limits, keep Server Timeout Header Limits connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A review team handles case 9993 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
